title: Repo-Profile Skill Binding Frontmatter Schema
description: Validates the smart-loading frontmatter fields on every SKILL.md under .claude/skills/**. These fields drive PLAN-083 Wave 0b sub-agent 0.7d (smart-loading-resolver.py) and the per-profile active-set caps (frontend <=10, engine <=12, fintech <=15, trading-readonly <=8, generic <=6). This schema validates ONLY the smart-loading subset of frontmatter; it intentionally allows other pre-existing fields (name, description, owner, inspired_by, etc.) via additionalProperties:true. The smart-loading subset is required; everything else is left alone for backward compatibility with the 143 existing SKILL.md files.

repo_profile_binding.description: Per-repo-profile binding map. Keys are the 5 canonical profile names defined by sub-agent 0.6 (detect-repo-profile.py) plus the catch-all 'generic'. Each value declares whether this skill auto-activates for that profile and a profile-local priority tie-breaker. Profiles not listed are implicitly inactive for this skill.

activation_triggers.description: Optional list of triggers that signal this skill should be considered for activation in addition to its repo_profile_binding. The resolver evaluates these triggers against the current event stream (file edits, repo scan results, /help-me invocations). Empty/absent array means the skill activates purely from repo_profile_binding.active=true. Per Wave 0b duplicate-trigger arbitration, when 2+ skills match the same trigger, ties are broken by top-level priority, then risk_class, then lexicographic skill path.

risk_class.description: Risk classification for duplicate-trigger arbitration tie-break #2. Lower risk wins to favor vibecoder ergonomics (less likely to surprise the user with unexpected guardrails). 'low' = read-only / advisory / docs / scaffolding; 'medium' = mutates code or config in well-scoped ways; 'high' = security-sensitive, trading-path, PII, or anything that can cause hard-to-reverse damage. The cap-table arbitration in smart-loading-resolver.py uses this as tie-break #2 after the top-level priority field.

domain.description: Framework domain slug this skill belongs to. Mirrors the directory under .claude/skills/. Examples: 'core', 'frontend', 'fintech', 'lgpd-heavy-saas', 'trading-hft', 'edtech', 'government', 'community'. Used by audit-query.py by-domain (S100 deliverable) and by smart-loading-resolver.py for per-profile budget accounting.

stack.description: Optional list of technology stack hints. Lowercase tokens preferred. Examples: 'typescript', 'react', 'nextjs', 'python', 'fastapi', 'postgres', 'redis', 'node', 'go', 'rust'. The resolver uses these to refine activation when sub-agent 0.6 has tagged the repo with a stack manifest; absent stack means the skill is stack-agnostic.

inactive_but_retained.description: Owner directive (PLAN-083 §4 'do NOT cut skills/bundles'): when true, the skill is preserved in the repo for forensic/historical/Owner-override use but is auto-suppressed from the active set for ALL profiles regardless of repo_profile_binding.active. Only takes effect when set to true; the smart-loading-resolver MUST honor this flag and exclude the skill from active counts. Explicit Owner override (env var CEO_FORCE_SKILL=<name>) bypasses suppression.

context_budget_tokens.description: Estimated active-set token contribution for this skill (SKILL.md content body, excluding frontmatter). Used by smart-loading-resolver.py to enforce the Wave 0b acceptance criterion: SUM(context_budget_tokens) across active set per profile MUST be <=30000. Author should measure with token-estimator.py (sub-agent 0.2 deliverable) rather than guess. Max per-skill cap of 30k mirrors the per-profile total; in practice individual skills are 0.5-5k.

priority.description: Top-level priority for duplicate-trigger arbitration (PRIMARY key). 1 = highest precedence, 10 = lowest. When 2+ skills match the same activation trigger or compete for the same profile cap slot, the resolver picks the skill with the LOWEST priority integer. Tie-break #1 = risk_class (low > medium > high). Tie-break #2 = lexicographic skill path. Suggested anchors: 1-2 reserved for safety-critical (security-and-auth, anti-ceo-overhead); 3-4 = primary domain skills; 5-6 = standard skills; 7-8 = nice-to-have helpers; 9-10 = experimental/optional.

profile_binding_entry.description: Per-profile binding. Declares whether this skill auto-activates for the given repo profile and the profile-local priority used only when comparing two skills both matching the same profile (NOT the same global trigger).

active.description: When true, this skill is a candidate for inclusion in this profile's active set (subject to the per-profile cap and global context_budget_tokens). When false, the skill is bound to this profile for documentation/discovery purposes but does NOT consume a cap slot.

priority.description: Profile-local priority (1=highest, 10=lowest). Used as a sub-key after the top-level priority field when ranking candidates for inclusion under this specific profile. Allows the same skill to be 'priority 2 for frontend, priority 6 for fintech' without duplicating the SKILL.md.

activation_trigger.description: A single trigger that signals this skill should be considered for activation. The resolver evaluates triggers against the live event stream; matched triggers add the skill to the candidate pool for the current profile, then cap/arbitration rules decide final inclusion.

glob.description: Optional file-path glob (POSIX glob syntax) evaluated against the path involved in the triggering event. Examples: 'src/**/*.ts', 'package.json', '.github/workflows/*.yml'. Absent glob means the trigger fires on any path.

regex.description: Optional Python re.search-compatible regex evaluated against the content of the file or the body of the event payload. Use sparingly: regex triggers are O(N*M) and can blow the resolver's <50ms p95 budget. Absent regex means no content match required.

event.description: The event class that fires this trigger. 'file-edit' = PreToolUse Edit/Write. 'repo-detect' = sub-agent 0.6 detect-repo-profile.py result. 'help-me-invoked' = /help me slash command. 'session-boot' = ceo-boot.py run at session start. Adding new event types requires extending sub-agent 0.7d resolver + this enum atomically.
